title: Fix 'Enter The Realm' button — dead click handler in CharacterSelectScreen

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Enter The Realm' button click handler in CharacterSelectScreen.tsx to ensure it fires onSelectCharacter callback and produces immediate feedback
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Enter The Realm' with a character highlighted produces an immediate navigation response — no blank screen, no spinner, no silence.
The onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent prop) is invoked with the correct character ID on every click.
The button is never silently disabled or pointer-events-blocked when a character is selected.
No click event is swallowed by a parent element.
After the callback fires, the app transitions to the character sheet / dungeon select screen without requiring an additional user action.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the 'Enter The Realm' button's onClick handler end-to-end. Ensure: (1) the button has a valid onClick handler that is not conditionally removed; (2) the handler invokes onSelectCharacter (or equivalent prop) with selectedCharacter's ID when a character is selected; (3) no parent element has pointer-events-none or stopPropagation that would swallow clicks; (4) selectedCharacter state is non-null before the callback is invoked; (5) remove any guard conditions or early returns that silently prevent the callback from firing. Verify the button is never conditionally disabled when a valid character is selected.

requirements.summary: Fix the onSelectCharacter callback in App.tsx to ensure it correctly updates activeCharacterId state and immediately transitions routing without being blocked
acceptanceCriteria: onSelectCharacter is never undefined when CharacterSelectScreen renders.
Invoking the callback sets activeCharacterId in App state to the correct character ID.
The routing state transitions to the character sheet / dungeon select screen synchronously after the callback fires.
No loading flag or guard condition remains true and blocks the transition.
The transition occurs on the first click without needing a second attempt or page reload.

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent) passed to CharacterSelectScreen. Ensure: (1) the callback is defined as a stable function using useCallback or equivalent and is never undefined at render time; (2) it correctly updates activeCharacterId (or equivalent App-level state) to the selected character's ID; (3) it immediately updates the routing/screen state to transition to the character sheet or dungeon select screen after setting the ID; (4) no isLoading flag, loading guard, or conditional render blocks the screen transition after the callback completes; (5) if any guard exists, confirm it evaluates to false (non-blocking) by the time the callback finishes executing. The transition must occur synchronously on the first click.
